summary: Map the whole accuracy-vs-cost Pareto frontier and let a human pick the operating point.
_doc: Objective template consumed by MetaHarnessCoordinator + templated into the proposer prompt. The default objective: freeform multi-objective optimization (the paper's posture). The proposer is told to surface candidates across the whole frontier (cheap-but-good through expensive-but-best), not chase a single scalar. PR-eligible frontier knees are labeled by operating point.

objective.target: Find harness-content edits that expand the accuracy-vs-cost Pareto frontier vs the current baseline. Prefer surgical, single-hypothesis edits. A candidate is interesting if it (a) holds land-rate while cutting context tokens, OR (b) lifts land-rate at modest token cost.
objective.proposer_hint: Read the raw traces to find where context tokens are spent for little return (cost wins) AND where missing/mis-prioritized context correlates with low rubric scores or failed renders (accuracy wins). Across iterations, deliberately probe BOTH ends of the frontier: some candidates that aggressively trim, some that add targeted context.

frontier_knees.label: cost-optimal
frontier_knees.rule: lowest mean_input_tokens among non-dominated candidates that match baseline land-rate within keep_bar epsilon

frontier_knees.label: balanced
frontier_knees.rule: the non-dominated candidate with the best land-rate-per-token ratio
